translation: The Gracious One
HE has given the knowledge of the Qur’aan,
notes: And in Verse 15:9 Allah Almighty has promised to guard the Qur’aan. So, every believer in Alllah and His Book, the Qur’aan, has necessarily to believe that the contents of the Book are cent per cent TRUE.

translation: HE has created mankind.
He has taught mankind the art and skill of communicating with one another.
[HE has created] the sun and the moon on accurately measured dimentions, properties and positions.
notes: Had those dimensions, properties and positions been not as they are, no life on earth, as we know it, would be possible.

translation: And the star and the tree prostrate!
notes: As I was studying this Verse aloud, my wife exclaimed, “But the star and the tree do not prostrate!” I explained that the word ‘prostrate’ symbolizes complete submission. The star symbolizes all the heavenly bodies – including the Sun – in the sky. The Sun, as we know, serves as the fountain-head of all life on this earth. The vegetable section – represented by ‘the tree’ – of the life on this planet serves by providing food and other necessities for sustenance of life here. Both these sections of Creation unquestioningly serve their Creator and thus prostrate to Him. It is only man who may literally – but not spiritually – prostrate to Him!

translation: And the sky He has raised high and set up the balance .
So that you breach not that balance!
notes: Refer study note 2 on Verse 51:7. However, the Arabic word almeezan used here could as well mean the tool used for weighing/measuring things. That is the meaning in which the word is used in Verse 9 that follows.

arabic: وَأَقِيمُواْ ٱلۡوَزۡنَ بِٱلۡقِسۡطِ وَلَا تُخۡسِرُواْ ٱلۡمِيزَانَ
transliteration: Waaqeemoo alwazna bialqisti wala tukhsiroo almeezana
translation: And weigh things correctly and cut not the measure short!
And He has made the earth suitable for living beings
Therein is fruit, and the palm tree with its sheathed clusters of dates
And the grain in its husk, and the plants giving out fragrance.
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
He has created mankind of clay, as [mankind makes] pottery [out of it].

arabic: وَخَلَقَ ٱلۡجَآنَّ مِن مَّارِجٖ مِّن نَّارٖ
transliteration: Wakhalaqa aljanna min marijin min narin
translation: And He has created the invisible beings [jinns] out of radiation from fire.
notes: Mankind and other living creatures on the earth are made of matter and therefore can be seen. Even the microbes can be seen under a microscope. But not the jinns! They are made of energy. A fire can be seen, but not its radiation.

translation: So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
Lord of the two Easts, and Lord of the two Wests
notes: We know what the East and the West for mankind is. The other East and the other West referred to here is apparently for the Jinns. We do not know what those are.

translation: So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
He has made the two streams of water flow side by side
Between them is a barrier which they do not transgress.
notes: In Brazil, two rivers, black-coloured Rio Nego and sandy-coloured Amazon run side by side without mixing for 6 kms. [Source]. There are other such examples in the world.

translation: So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
Out of these two come forth pearls, and precious corals.
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?

arabic: وَلَهُ ٱلۡجَوَارِ ٱلۡمُنشَـَٔاتُ فِي ٱلۡبَحۡرِ كَٱلۡأَعۡلَٰمِ
transliteration: Walahu aljawari almunshaatu fee albahri kaalaAAlami
translation: And it is He Who has made it possible for the ships to remain afloat like some signboards raised in the sea.
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
Everything [every ship] thereon [on the sea] is passing
notes: Like the passing ship on the sea, existence of everything in the Universe – except Allah’s – is temporary.

arabic: وَيَبۡقَىٰ وَجۡهُ رَبِّكَ ذُو ٱلۡجَلَٰلِ وَٱلۡإِكۡرَامِ
transliteration: Wayabqa wajhu rabbika thoo aljalali waalikrami
translation: And, what is lasting is the Presence of your Lord, Majestic and Magnanimous!
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?

arabic: يَسۡـَٔلُهُۥ مَن فِي ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِۚ كُلَّ يَوۡمٍ هُوَ فِي شَأۡنٖ
transliteration: Yasaluhu man fee alssamawati waalardi kulla yawmin huwa fee shanin
translation: Everything in the heavens and on earth takes its orders from Him. Every day it is He Who manifests Himself gloriously [and there is none to overcome His Power!]
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
We shall soon attend to you, O you sin-laden two [mankind and jinn]!
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?

arabic: يَٰمَعۡشَرَ ٱلۡجِنِّ وَٱلۡإِنسِ إِنِ ٱسۡتَطَعۡتُمۡ أَن تَنفُذُواْ مِنۡ أَقۡطَارِ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ فَٱنفُذُواْۚ لَا تَنفُذُونَ إِلَّا بِسُلۡطَٰنٖ
transliteration: Ya maAAshara aljinni waalinsi ini istataAAtum an tanfuthoo min aqtari alssamawati waalardi faonfuthoo la tanfuthoona illa bisultanin
translation: O assembly of the jinn and mankind! If you think you can pass beyond the frontiers of the heavens and the earth, then pass! You cannot pass beyond, save by a sanction [from Allah] !
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?

arabic: يُرۡسَلُ عَلَيۡكُمَا شُوَاظٞ مِّن نَّارٖ وَنُحَاسٞ فَلَا تَنتَصِرَانِ
transliteration: Yursalu AAalaykuma shuwathun min narin wanuhasun fala tantasirani
translation: A trail of fire and smoke will be sent upon you, and you both will be left with no help!
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?

arabic: فَإِذَا ٱنشَقَّتِ ٱلسَّمَآءُ فَكَانَتۡ وَرۡدَةٗ كَٱلدِّهَانِ
transliteration: Faitha inshaqqati alssamao fakanat wardatan kaalddihani
translation: Then as the sky disintegrates it would appear a rose-coloured stretch of leather.
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?

arabic: فَيَوۡمَئِذٖ لَّا يُسۡـَٔلُ عَن ذَنۢبِهِۦٓ إِنسٞ وَلَا جَآنّٞ
transliteration: Fayawmaithin la yusalu AAan thanbihi insun wala jannun
translation: And on that Day none – neither mankind nor jinn – will be questioned about his/her sins.
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?

arabic: يُعۡرَفُ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمُونَ بِسِيمَٰهُمۡ فَيُؤۡخَذُ بِٱلنَّوَٰصِي وَٱلۡأَقۡدَامِ
transliteration: YuAArafu almujrimoona biseemahum fayukhathu bialnnawasee waalaqdami
translation: All the sinners shall be recognised by distinguishing marks on them and seized by their forelocks and their feet!
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?

arabic: هَٰذِهِۦ جَهَنَّمُ ٱلَّتِي يُكَذِّبُ بِهَا ٱلۡمُجۡرِمُونَ
transliteration: Hathihi jahannamu allatee yukaththibu biha almujrimoona
translation: This is the Hell that the sinners had dismissed as being a lie:
They wander to and fro between it [burning hot flame] and burning-hot water!
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
And for those who fear their Lord’s [Invincible] Position, two Gardens [of Paradise, each for mankind and jinn]
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
[The two Gardens, each] having sections of various kinds.
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
In those two [gardens] flow two springs.
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
In those two [gardens] are pairs of every fruit.
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?

arabic: مُتَّكِـِٔينَ عَلَىٰ فُرُشِۭ بَطَآئِنُهَا مِنۡ إِسۡتَبۡرَقٖۚ وَجَنَى ٱلۡجَنَّتَيۡنِ دَانٖ
transliteration: Muttakieena AAala furushin batainuha min istabraqin wajana aljannatayni danin
translation: [The blessed inhabitants of the gardens] recline upon carpets lined with rich brocade; and the fruit of both those gardens is within easy reach.
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?

arabic: فِيهِنَّ قَٰصِرَٰتُ ٱلطَّرۡفِ لَمۡ يَطۡمِثۡهُنَّ إِنسٞ قَبۡلَهُمۡ وَلَا جَآنّٞ
transliteration: Feehinna qasiratu alttarfi lam yatmithhunna insun qablahum wala jannun
translation: In those [gardens] are mates of modest gaze, whom neither man nor any jinn had touched ever before.
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
[Those mates are] as though they were rubies and precious corals.
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
Could the reward for good deeds be anything but good?
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
And there are two gardens besides those two.
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
Both [gardens] deep green in colour!
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
In those two [gardens], two gushing springs!
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
In both [gardens], fruits, dates and pomegranates!
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
In these [gardens] are things excellent and beautiful.
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
[In those gardens is companionship of] modest Houris in pavilions
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?

arabic: لَمۡ يَطۡمِثۡهُنَّ إِنسٞ قَبۡلَهُمۡ وَلَا جَآنّٞ
transliteration: Lam yatmithhunna insun qablahum wala jannun
translation: [Houris] whom neither man nor any jinn had ever touched before
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?

arabic: مُتَّكِـِٔينَ عَلَىٰ رَفۡرَفٍ خُضۡرٖ وَعَبۡقَرِيٍّ حِسَانٖ
transliteration: Muttakieena AAala rafrafin khudrin waAAabqariyyin hisanin
translation: [Dwellers of Paradise] recline upon green meadows and beautiful carpets
So, which of your Lord´s Means of Power will you deny?
notes: This refrain in this Chapter highlights things, both here and in the Hereafter, which none but Allah Almighty can do, bring about or create. It highlights the fact that despite these divine provisions, man remains ungrateful to his Lord, Creator and Provider!

arabic: تَبَٰرَكَ ٱسۡمُ رَبِّكَ ذِي ٱلۡجَلَٰلِ وَٱلۡإِكۡرَامِ
transliteration: Tabaraka ismu rabbika thee aljalali waalikrami
translation: Blessed is the Name of your Lord, Majestic and Glorified!
